max_flow_value: Using the capacities on the directed edges, what is the maximum flow from source S to sink T?
What is the largest total flow that can be sent from S to T without exceeding any edge capacity?
Find the max-flow value from source S to sink T in this capacity network.
Respecting the arrow capacities, how much flow can be sent from S to T at most?
What maximum S-to-T flow value does the directed capacity network allow?
minimum_cut_edge_count: For the source S to sink T flow network, how many directed edges are in the unique minimum cut?
How many edges cross the unique minimum S-T cut in this capacity network?
Find the unique minimum cut separating S from T. How many directed edges are in that cut?
What is the number of directed edges in the minimum-capacity cut from S to T?
Count the directed edges whose capacities make up the unique minimum S-T cut. How many are there?

query:max_flow_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a list of segments. Each segment is [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]], where each endpoint is an [x, y] pixel point at a node center of one directed edge in a minimum S-T cut supporting the max-flow value
query:max_flow_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the maximum flow value as an integer

query:minimum_cut_edge_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a list of segments. Each segment is [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]], where each endpoint is an [x, y] pixel point at a node center of one directed edge in the unique minimum S-T cut
query:minimum_cut_edge_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the number of directed edges in the unique minimum S-T cut as an integer

object_description.description: Generated description of the rendered flow-network diagram.
